content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
January 6, 1989 
A. Kay Vinson 
City Clerk 
City of Barstow 
220 East Mountain View street 
Barstow, CA 
92311-9981 
Re: 
Our File No. G-89-020 
Dear Ms. Vinson: 
We have reviewed the draft informational brochure prepared 
by your office. 
Our only comment is that the address and 
telephone number for filing complaints with the Commission 
should be as follows: 
Enforcement Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
(916) 322-6441 
Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if I may be of further 
assistance. 
Sincerely, 
« 
c 
I -.(~ L ~tA-{'VT~ 
Kathryn~. Donovan 
Counsel, Legal Division 
KED:plh 
t 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


The 
C 
I Y 
o f 
California 
December 29, 1988 
Fair Political Practices commission 
428 J st., suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Sir or Madam: 
Pursuant to direction by the Barstow city Council, I am 
preparing an informational brochure where complaints may be 
filed against public officials. 
I would appreciate your 
review of the section relative to the Fair Political 
Practices commission and your response with suggestions for 
changes or approval. 
If no response is received by January 
26, 1989, it will be considered consent for usage as 
presented. 
Best wishes for a great 1989. 
Sincerely yours, 
E"

Pone (619) 263 


What does the SECRETARY 
OF STATE do? 
The Secretary of State is the chief elections 
officer of the state, and shall administer the 
provisions of the Elections Code. The 
Secretary of State shall see that elections are 
efficiently conducted and that state election 
laws are enforced. Among other duties the 
Secretary of State is the custodian and 
record keeper of many official state records. 
Complaints regarding the conduct of elec-
tions may be filed with the: 
Secretary of State 
Elections Division 
1230 J St. 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Authority: G.c. 12150-12260

sections.conclusion: Our only comment is that the address and telephone number for filing complaints with the Commission should be as follows: Enforcement Division, Fair Political Practices Commission, , (916) 322-6441
sections.question_synthetic: What address and contact information should be provided in an informational brochure about filing complaints with the FPPC?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided the correct Enforcement Division address and telephone number that should be included in the City of Barstow's informational brochure for filing complaints.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a straightforward response letter with incoming request included. The document contains both the incoming request from City of Barstow (dated December 29, 1988) and the FPPC's response (dated January 6, 1989). No formal Question and Conclusion sections are present, but the substance is clear from the letter's content.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What address and contact information should be provided in an informational brochure about filing complaints with the FPPC?

CONCLUSION: Our only comment is that the address and telephone number for filing complaints with the Commission should be as follows: Enforcement Division, Fair Political Practices Commission, , (916) 322-6441

embedding.summary: The FPPC reviewed a draft informational brochure prepared by the City of Barstow regarding where complaints against public officials may be filed, and provided the correct contact information for the FPPC's Enforcement Division to be included in the brochure.
